title: lepidium montanum subsp. spathulatum
intro: Lepidium montanum subsp. spathulatum, also known as Mountain Pepper Cress or Western Peppergrass, is a perennial plant in the mustard family native to the western coast of North America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high levels of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Lepidium montanum subsp. spathulatum can be prepared as an herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used as a culinary ingredient for its peppery flavor, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal and health benefits.
